purpose: Stage-2 cold-PATH install proof: proves the release-installed git-lex and git-lex-serve resolve through an explicit cold PATH (no vendor-dir) from /tmp, the git 'lex' subcommand dispatches to the installed git-lex binary, --version exits 2 (version gap, no version claim), and the main law-nexus checkout has no R047 residue before or after. Second empirical install-proof (resolution through PATH; binary identity is T01's install-manifest.json).

cold_path_definition.note: explicit cold PATH containing ~/.cargo/bin and standard system dirs; does NOT contain /root/vendor-source/git-lex, /root/vendor-source/git-lex/target/debug, or any source/target path

path_resolution.evidence: command -v under cold env resolves both binaries to the cargo bin dir, matching the T01 install-manifest.json binary paths

git_lex_direct_help.evidence: direct binary resolution through cold PATH; the installed git-lex ran and printed its banner

git_lex_serve_help.evidence: direct binary resolution through cold PATH (S01 contract section 5 item 3 satisfied)

git_lex_help_via_dispatch.note: git intercepts --help for EXTERNAL subcommands and routes it to man(1); with no man page installed, this prints 'No manual entry for git-lex' and exits non-zero (observed rc=16). This is git's documented external-subcommand help-dispatch behavior, NOT an install defect, and it proves git recognizes 'lex' as a dispatchable subcommand (a non-existent subcommand yields rc=1 'is not a git command' — see contrast). The banner is NOT shown via this path because git never passes --help to the external binary. Contract section 5 item 2 ('git lex --help exits 0') assumed pass-through; the actual git dispatch routes --help to man. The cold-PATH resolution + dispatch INTENT is proven by git_lex_direct_help + git_subcommand_dispatch.

git_subcommand_dispatch.primary_evidence: git found git-lex via cold-PATH lookup and dispatched the 'lex' subcommand; git-lex ran with no args, so clap printed the banner + usage and exited 2 (missing required subcommand). Banner presence proves the installed git-lex binary executed (S01 contract section 5 item 4 satisfied).
git_subcommand_dispatch.secondary_evidence: clap error 'unexpected argument --version found ... Usage: git-lex <COMMAND>' references git-lex, proving the dispatched binary was the installed git-lex.
git_subcommand_dispatch.contrast_evidence: non-existent subcommand -> rc=1 'is not a git command'; 'lex' dispatches (rc=2, banner present), confirming lex IS a resolved subcommand, not a not-found case.

version_gap.note: contract prohibits version claim; --version exits 2 per M051/S09 and re-proven here. No version number is parsed or asserted.
